turnedAway.note: People turned away at polling stations for not having accepted photo ID. 2024 figure lower partly due to greater awareness and Voter Authority Certificate uptake.

voterAwareness.note: Percentage of registered voters who could correctly identify which forms of photo ID are accepted at polling stations.

idPossessionGaps.note: Estimated percentage of registered voters in each group lacking any accepted form of photo ID, 2024. Electoral Commission survey.

voterAuthorityCertificates.note: Free Voter Authority Certificates issued by councils to those without qualifying photo ID.

sources.name: Electoral Commission
sources.dataset: May 2023 Local Elections — Voter ID Research

sources.name: Electoral Commission
sources.dataset: 2024 UK General Election — Voter ID Monitoring Report

sources.name: ONS
sources.dataset: Electoral Integrity — Voter ID possession survey

metadata.methodology: Turned away figures from Electoral Commission post-election monitoring, based on polling station returns. Awareness figures from Electoral Commission pre-election survey. ID possession gaps from ONS electoral integrity survey matched with demographic data. VAC figures from MHCLG local authority returns.
knownIssues: Turned away figures include those who returned with correct ID and voted — net disenfranchisement is lower but unknown
ID possession surveys are pre-election estimates — not all those without ID would have voted anyway
2024 general election saw higher turnout among young voters than 2023 locals, complicating like-for-like comparisons
Voter Authority Certificate take-up concentrated in areas with active council outreach programmes
